code: mpg |>   ggauto(displ) ggauto(mpg, displ) ggauto(mpg$displ) mpg |>   ggauto(drv, displ)

title: Examples
text: single variable show time, .e., one date variable, one continuous variable:  line chart produced. need show multiple variables change time, .e., one date variable, continuous variable, one discrete variable, type chart depend many categories (unique values discrete variable) . 6 fewer categories, multi-line chart created, colours symbols identifying categories. Category labels added end line automatically.  6 categories, plot type changes faceted line chart, one category highlighted facet:
code: economics |>   ggauto(date, unemploy) txhousing |>   dplyr::filter(city %in% c("Houston", "Fort Worth", "San Antonio", "Austin")) |>   dplyr::mutate(date = lubridate::ymd(paste0(year, "/", month, "/01"))) |>   ggauto(date, sales, city) txhousing |>   dplyr::filter(city %in% c(     "Houston", "Fort Worth", "San Antonio", "Austin",     "Bay Area", "Dallas", "Paris", "San Angelo"   )) |>   dplyr::mutate(date = lubridate::ymd(paste0(year, "/", month, "/01"))) |>   ggauto(date, sales, city)

title: Examples
text: single discrete variable, bar chart showing counts category created:  pre-computed counts summary statistics, .e., one discrete variable, one continuous variable single value discrete variable, bar chart values created:  can see, discrete variable factor (.e. cut), desired order respected. discrete variable factor, bars ordered highest lowest instead default alphabetical ordering:  two discrete variables, heatmap created showing count combination categories. Labels added showing count.  6 categories either axis, labels replaced legend:  , one discrete variables factor, order respected:  two discrete variables third continuous variable showing summary statistic category combination, heatmap showing value created. Labels rounded 2 decimal places.  multiple continuous values per combination categories, error returned, asking first summarise data:
code: diamonds |>   ggauto(cut) diamonds |>   dplyr::group_by(cut) |>   dplyr::summarise(median_price = median(price)) |>   ggauto(cut, median_price) diamonds |>   dplyr::group_by(cut) |>   dplyr::summarise(median_price = median(price)) |>   dplyr::mutate(cut = as.character(cut)) |>   ggauto(cut, median_price) mpg |>   dplyr::mutate(cyl = as.character(cyl)) |>   ggauto(cyl, drv) mpg |>   dplyr::mutate(cyl = as.character(cyl)) |>   ggauto(trans, cyl) mpg |>   dplyr::mutate(cyl = as.factor(cyl)) |>   ggauto(trans, cyl) mpg |>   dplyr::mutate(cyl = as.character(cyl)) |>   dplyr::group_by(cyl, drv) |>   dplyr::summarise(mean_hwy = mean(hwy), .groups = "drop") |>   ggauto(cyl, drv, mean_hwy) mpg |>   dplyr::mutate(cyl = as.character(cyl)) |>   ggauto(cyl, drv, hwy) #> Error in `ggauto()`: #> ! Too many values per category. Summarise data first.

code: set.seed(123) plot_data <- data.frame(   v1 = rnorm(50, 1) ) ggauto(plot_data, v1) +   scale_x_continuous() #> Scale for x is already present. #> Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Examples
text: can title, subtitle, caption, labels labs() function ggplot2 normally , directly using arguments ggauto(). latter recommended arguments used little abnormally implement styling. can add markdown formatting title, subtitle, caption:  default, x y axis title removed chart types e.g. axis date category label stating unnecessary. Unless otherwise specified, axis labels clean versions column names ’s parsed sentence case, underscores removed. can edit size family text using base_size base_family arguments. plot elements e.g. lines points scale relative base_size well.
code: plot_data |>   ggauto(v1,     title = "Descriptive title goes here",     subtitle = "More information about what's in the chart which can be a really, really long sentence that will wrap onto multiple lines automatically.",     caption = "**Source**: where the data is from",     xlab = "Nice variable name"   )

what: Automatically Create and Style ggplot2 Charts
title: Automatically Create and Style ggplot2 Charts
text: ggauto opinionated ggplot2 extension package automatically choose best chart type styling, based types values data. ’s based following three principles: automatically perfect chart type given data type, better others. Default styling charts improve clarity accessible possible. Data pre-processing separate step plotting, need understand data plot . Warning! don’t like () opinionated choices package, make fork create version. Bug reports /fixes extremely welcome things don’t work, stylistic changes personal preferences addressed. package built philosophy data wrangling plotting separate parts process building chart. Tasks like ordering data, converting correct date formats, computing summary statistics generally performed passing plotting function. terms styling, defaults differ ggplot2 following ways: use white background improve contrast. Larger text aligned horizontally improve readability. Improved styling title subtitle, including automatic text wrapping long text. Colours likely accessible, using Paul Tol’s palettes. Combined use either shapes direct labels alongside colour improve accessibility. Symmetric y axis, 0 included data (chart types), enable comparison. Different chart types users try make spaghetti line charts. Unless factor specific order defined, categorical variables arranged magnitude instead alphabetically.

title: Automated ggplot2 charts — ggauto
text: Automatically create appropriate 'ggplot2' chart type based classes provided variables.

title: Automated ggplot2 charts — ggauto
code: ggauto(   data = NULL,   ...,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   base_size = 14,   base_family = "sans" )

title: Automated ggplot2 charts — ggauto
text: data data frame, bare vector using pipe. ... Unquoted column names plot (e.g. v1, v2). xlab Label x-axis. ylab Label y-axis. title Optional plot title. subtitle Optional plot subtitle. caption Optional plot caption. base_size Base font size. Defaults 14. base_family Base font family. Defaults "sans".
